title: Simpsons Roasting On An Open Fire

jokes.text: Ooh! Careful, Homer! There's no time. We're late.
jokes.setup: Family rushing to get somewhere important
jokes.punchline: Homer being characteristically clumsy/careless while they're already late
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer characterization - clumsy and oblivious to urgency. Well-integrated character moment.

jokes.text: How still we see the elie
jokes.setup: Children singing traditional Christmas carol 'O Little Town of Bethlehem'
jokes.punchline: Child mispronouncing 'thee' as 'elie'
jokes.explanation: Simple child mispronunciation gag - predictable but effective for the setting.

jokes.text: Hey, Norman, how's it goin'? So you got dragged down here too, huh?
jokes.setup: Parents at school Christmas pageant
jokes.punchline: Parents treating mandatory school event as mutual suffering
jokes.explanation: Relatable parental experience - the shared misery of school events. Good observational humor.

jokes.text: Pardon my galoshes.
jokes.setup: Parent navigating crowded auditorium
jokes.punchline: Overly polite/formal language for stepping on someone
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective formal language contrast. Economy of language works well.

jokes.text: In Germany, Santa's servant Ruprecht gives presents to good children... and whipping rods to the parents of bad ones.
jokes.setup: Educational presentation about international Christmas traditions
jokes.punchline: Dark twist - parents get punished, not the bad children
jokes.explanation: Clever subversion - parents punished instead of children. Dark humor that's both educational and funny.

jokes.text: I have eyes in the back of my head... so children better behave when I'm nearby.
jokes.setup: Japanese priest character presentation
jokes.punchline: Taking parental saying literally as supernatural power
jokes.explanation: Child's literal interpretation of common parental threat creates absurd supernatural claim.

jokes.text: Ooh, it's Lisa! That's ours.
jokes.setup: Parents watching school pageant
jokes.punchline: Homer's proud but possessive identification of his child
jokes.explanation: Homer's simple parental pride expressed in characteristically blunt way.

jokes.text: Uh, medley of holiday 'flavorites.'
jokes.setup: Announcer introducing musical performance
jokes.punchline: Mispronunciation of 'favorites' as 'flavorites'
jokes.explanation: Simple malapropism - teacher trying to sound fancy but messing up the word.

jokes.text: Oh, jingle bells Batman smells Robin laid an egg The Batmobile broke its wheel The joker got awa--
jokes.setup: Children's choir singing traditional Jingle Bells
jokes.punchline: Bart singing the subversive playground version instead
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart moment - subverting formal event with playground humor. Highly quotable and character-defining.

jokes.text: Isn't Bart sweet, Homer? He sings like an angel.
jokes.setup: Marge commenting on Bart's singing
jokes.punchline: Calling him angelic right as he's being subversive
jokes.explanation: Perfect dramatic irony - Marge's innocent comment contrasts with Bart's misbehavior.

jokes.text: How many grades does this school have?
jokes.setup: Multiple grade levels performing at Christmas pageant
jokes.punchline: Homer's exasperated realization the show is endless
jokes.explanation: Relatable parent frustration - school events that drag on forever. Homer's impatience is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Our little cat Snowball... was unexpectedly run over and went to kitty heaven. But we bought a new little cat, Snowball I.
jokes.setup: Family Christmas letter sharing news
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of pet death followed by numbering error for replacement
jokes.explanation: Dark humor mixed with absurdist logic error. The numbering mistake adds to the casual treatment of pet death.

jokes.text: Maggie is walking by herself, Lisa got straight A's, and Bart-- Well, we love Bart.
jokes.setup: Christmas letter listing children's accomplishments
jokes.punchline: Unable to find anything positive to say about Bart
jokes.explanation: Perfect parental diplomacy - finding the most positive thing you can say. Economy of language is excellent.

jokes.text: Marge, haven't you finished that stupid letter yet?
jokes.setup: Marge writing heartfelt Christmas letter
jokes.punchline: Homer calling it stupid while being included in it
jokes.explanation: Homer's typical insensitivity - dismissing something that includes praise for him.

jokes.text: Sorry. I'm just a big kid. And I love Christmas so much.
jokes.setup: Homer being impatient about Christmas preparations
jokes.punchline: Self-aware admission of childlike behavior
jokes.explanation: Homer showing rare self-awareness about his childlike enthusiasm.

jokes.text: D'oh!
jokes.setup: Homer doing something with Christmas decorations
jokes.punchline: Homer's signature exclamation of frustration
jokes.explanation: Homer's iconic catchphrase - one of the most quotable and recognizable TV lines ever created.

jokes.text: Oh, please. There's only one fat guy that brings us presents, and his name ain't Santa.
jokes.setup: Teacher talking about sending letters to Santa
jokes.punchline: Bart's cynical reference to Homer being the real gift-giver
jokes.explanation: Bart's street-smart cynicism about Santa, while also affectionately acknowledging Homer's role.

jokes.text: Oh, Lisa, you've asked for that for the last three years, and I keep telling you Santa can't fit a pony into his sleigh. Can't you take a hint?
jokes.setup: Lisa asking for a pony again
jokes.punchline: Homer's increasingly desperate excuse-making about Santa's logistics
jokes.explanation: Homer's parental frustration with repeated impossible requests, using increasingly absurd Santa logistics.

jokes.text: A tattoo? A what? Yeah! They're cool, and they last the rest of your life.
jokes.setup: Bart revealing his Christmas wish
jokes.punchline: Bart's childish excitement about tattoo permanence horrifying Marge
jokes.explanation: Bart's innocent enthusiasm about something permanently inappropriate creates perfect parent horror.

jokes.text: Yeah. If you want one, you'll have to pay for it out of your allowance. All right!
jokes.setup: Homer trying to discourage tattoo by mentioning cost
jokes.punchline: Bart's enthusiasm is undeterred by financial responsibility
jokes.explanation: Homer's attempt at practical deterrent backfires as Bart sees it as validation.

jokes.text: Somehow I doubt that Homer is excited. of all the men you could've married, I don't know why you picked one who's always so rude to us.
jokes.setup: Patty talking to Marge about Homer
jokes.punchline: Sister's direct criticism of Homer's attitude toward them
jokes.explanation: Classic in-law dynamic - sister expressing disapproval of brother-in-law directly.

jokes.text: Good one, Dad.
jokes.setup: Homer presumably doing something impressive with Christmas lights
jokes.punchline: Bart's sarcastic approval of his father's effort
jokes.explanation: Simple sarcastic response to presumably underwhelming Christmas display.

jokes.text: What do you think, kids? Nice try, Dad.
jokes.setup: Homer showing off Christmas light display
jokes.punchline: Lisa's diplomatic but underwhelmed response
jokes.explanation: Lisa's characteristic diplomatic criticism - kinder than Bart but still clearly unimpressed.

jokes.text: Ho ho ho. Ho ho ho. Oh! Oh, neato! It's too bright. I oughta-- Flanders. What a big show-off.
jokes.setup: Flanders revealing elaborate Christmas display
jokes.punchline: Homer's immediate jealousy and dismissal of neighbor's superior decorations
jokes.explanation: Perfect neighbor rivalry - Homer's insecurity manifesting as dismissal of Flanders' success.

jokes.text: Turn around. you can look now. Ooh! Big jar this year.
jokes.setup: Marge revealing hidden Christmas money stash
jokes.punchline: Homer's childlike excitement about the money jar size
jokes.explanation: Homer's childlike excitement about money jar establishes their modest financial planning system.

jokes.text: Oh, Bart, that's so sweet. It's the best present a mother could get, and it makes you look so dangerous.
jokes.setup: Bart getting 'Mother' tattoo
jokes.punchline: Marge finding maternal tattoo both sweet and concerning for different reasons
jokes.explanation: Marge's conflicted reaction - maternal appreciation mixed with concern about Bart's image.

jokes.text: One 'Mother,' please. Wait a minute. How old are you? Twenty-one, sir.
jokes.setup: Bart trying to get tattoo
jokes.punchline: Obviously underage child claiming to be 21
jokes.explanation: Classic child attempting adult deception - predictable but in character for Bart.

jokes.text: However, for you semiskilled workers, there will be no Christmas bonuses. Oh, and one more thing. Merry Christmas!
jokes.setup: Burns announcing safety improvements don't affect management pay
jokes.punchline: Cheerfully wishing Merry Christmas right after canceling bonuses
jokes.explanation: Perfect Burns moment - cruel corporate decision delivered with fake cheer. Excellent character integration and dark humor.

jokes.text: Oh, thank God for the big jar.
jokes.setup: Homer learning about no Christmas bonus
jokes.punchline: Relief about secret money jar becomes tragically ironic
jokes.explanation: Callback gains dramatic irony knowing the jar money will be needed more than expected.

jokes.text: However, it is rather expensive, and we must insist on a cash payment up front. Cash? Mm-hmm. Thank God for Homer's Christmas bonus.
jokes.setup: Expensive tattoo removal requiring cash
jokes.punchline: Marge's relief about bonus that no longer exists
jokes.explanation: Dramatic irony builds as Marge references the bonus Homer lost, unaware of their financial crisis.

jokes.text: Ay, caramba! Now, whatever you do, don't squirm. you don't wanna get this sucker near your eye or your groin.
jokes.setup: Doctor warning about laser tattoo removal
jokes.punchline: Specific warning about sensitive body parts with dangerous laser
jokes.explanation: Bart's signature exclamation plus doctor's casual warning about painful laser consequences.

jokes.text: you don't wanna get this sucker near your eye or your groin.
jokes.setup: Doctor warning about laser safety
jokes.punchline: Specific mention of groin danger to a young boy
jokes.explanation: Mildly subversive medical humor. Adult concern mentioned to child character.

jokes.text: Ow! Quit it. Ow! Quit it.
jokes.setup: Bart undergoing painful tattoo removal
jokes.punchline: Bart talking to the laser as if it's a person bothering him
jokes.explanation: Bart's childlike response to pain - treating the laser like an annoying sibling.

jokes.text: The jar is empty! Oh, my God! We're ruined. Christmas is canceled. No presents for anyone!
jokes.setup: Homer discovering empty money jar
jokes.punchline: Immediate catastrophic thinking and dramatic pronouncement
jokes.explanation: Homer's tendency toward dramatic overreaction - from discovery to complete despair instantly.

jokes.text: Oh, yeah. My Christmas bonus. How silly of me. This'll be the best Christmas yet. The best any family ever had.
jokes.setup: Homer remembering he needs to tell Marge about no bonus
jokes.punchline: Overcompensating with impossibly optimistic promises
jokes.explanation: Homer's desperate overcompensation - making grand promises when he has no way to deliver.

jokes.text: I don't deserve you as much as a guy with a fat wallet... and a credit card that won't set off that horrible beeping.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to express his inadequacy to Marge
jokes.punchline: Specific reference to credit card decline sounds
jokes.explanation: Homer's self-deprecating honesty with specific, relatable detail about financial embarrassment.

jokes.text: Pantyhose. Practical and alluring. A six-pack. Oh! Only 4.99. Ooh! Pads of paper. I bet Bart can think of a million things to do with these.
jokes.setup: Homer shopping for Christmas presents with no money
jokes.punchline: Trying to find positive spin on obviously cheap, inadequate gifts
jokes.explanation: Homer's desperate attempts to rationalize terrible gifts shows his financial desperation and optimism.

jokes.text: A little squeak toy. It says it's for dogs, but she can't read.
jokes.setup: Homer finding gift for baby Maggie
jokes.punchline: Logic that baby won't know it's a dog toy because she's illiterate
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - technically correct but completely missing the point. Excellent economy and character integration.

jokes.text: Well, which ones are yours and which ones are mine? Well, let's see. Oh, this one's mine. This one's mine. This one's mine, and this-- They're all yours!
jokes.setup: Homer and Flanders sorting out mixed up shopping items
jokes.punchline: Flanders realizing all the cheap items belong to Homer
jokes.explanation: Social awkwardness as Flanders realizes Homer's financial situation through their contrasting purchases.

jokes.text: Hey, you dropped your pork chop. Gimme that!
jokes.setup: Flanders trying to help Homer with dropped item
jokes.punchline: Homer's desperate grab for his cheap meat
jokes.explanation: Homer's desperation shown through protective behavior over cheap food purchase.

jokes.text: What's the matter, Homer? Somebody leave a lump of coal in your stocking? You've been sitting there, sucking on a beer all day long. So? So, it's Christmas.
jokes.setup: Moe noticing Homer's Christmas depression
jokes.punchline: Homer's indifferent response to Christmas spirit criticism
jokes.explanation: Moe's attempt at Christmas spirit advice met with Homer's characteristic apathy.

jokes.text: I got me a part-timejob working as a Santa down at the mall. Wow! Can I do that?
jokes.setup: Barney explaining his mall Santa job
jokes.punchline: Homer's immediate interest in any money-making opportunity
jokes.explanation: Homer's desperation making him immediately interested in any job opportunity.

jokes.text: Do you like children? What do you mean? All the time? Even when they're nuts? Uh, I certainly do.
jokes.setup: Job interview question about liking children
jokes.punchline: Homer's confused clarification about when exactly he needs to like them
jokes.explanation: Homer's literal interpretation of job requirements shows his practical but limited understanding of childcare.

jokes.text: Um, Dasher. Dancer. Mm-hmm. Prancer. Mm-hmm. Nixon. Comet and... Cupid. Donna Dixon?
jokes.setup: Homer reciting reindeer names during training
jokes.punchline: Homer including President Nixon and actress Donna Dixon among reindeer
jokes.explanation: Homer's random cultural references mixed into reindeer names shows his scattered knowledge and poor preparation.

jokes.text: You're not really Santa, tubby. Why, you little egghead!
jokes.setup: Child confronting fake Santa Homer
jokes.punchline: Homer breaking character to argue with a child
jokes.explanation: Homer's inability to maintain professional composure when insulted by a child.

jokes.text: Hello, Patty. Hello, Selma. How was your trip? Fine. you both look well. Thank you. Yeah, well, Merry Christmas. It's Christmas?
jokes.setup: Forced pleasantries with visiting in-laws
jokes.punchline: Selma's sarcastic response to Homer's obvious discomfort
jokes.explanation: Awkward family dynamics as Homer tries to be polite but clearly wants to escape.

jokes.text: you wouldn't know it around here. And why is that? For one thing, there's no tree I was just on my way out to get one!
jokes.setup: Selma criticizing lack of Christmas preparations
jokes.punchline: Homer's defensive excuse-making and sudden tree mission
jokes.explanation: Homer's improvised excuse to escape criticism and family obligations.

jokes.text: So what do you think, kids? Beauty, isn't it? Why is there a birdhouse in it? Uh, that's an ornament.
jokes.setup: Homer presenting stolen/found tree to family
jokes.punchline: Tree has obvious birdhouse that Homer tries to pass off as decoration
jokes.explanation: Homer's weak attempt to explain away evidence of his questionable tree acquisition.

jokes.text: Do I smell gunpowder?
jokes.setup: Marge examining the suspicious Christmas tree
jokes.punchline: Implication that tree was acquired through violent means
jokes.explanation: Dark implication about Homer's tree acquisition methods delivered through Marge's innocent observation.

jokes.text: Hey, I couldn't afford lunch. Give me a bite of that donut.
jokes.setup: Homer working as mall Santa
jokes.punchline: Begging food from a child while in Santa costume
jokes.explanation: Homer's desperation breaking Santa character boundaries - asking children for food.

jokes.text: I hope you feel better, Santa. Oh, I will when Mrs. Claus' sisters get outta town.
jokes.setup: Child showing concern for sad Santa
jokes.punchline: Homer venting about in-laws to a child while in character
jokes.explanation: Brilliant blend of Homer's personal problems with Santa character - inappropriate but relatable adult complaint to child.

jokes.text: What's your name, Bart... ner? Uh, little partner? I'm Bart Simpson. Who are you? I'm jolly Old St. Nick.
jokes.setup: Homer as Santa meeting his own son
jokes.punchline: Nearly saying Bart's name before catching himself
jokes.explanation: Perfect dramatic irony moment - father and son not recognizing each other, with Homer almost blowing his cover.

jokes.text: I didn't know it was you. Nobody knows. It's a secret. I didn't get my bonus this year. But to keep the family from missing out on Christmas, I'd do anything.
jokes.setup: Homer explaining his situation to Bart
jokes.punchline: Homer's vulnerable admission of his desperation and love for family
jokes.explanation: Homer's rare moment of emotional honesty about his financial struggles and family devotion.

jokes.text: I'll say, Dad. you must really love us to sink so low.
jokes.setup: Bart understanding Homer's sacrifice
jokes.punchline: Bart's blunt assessment of how degrading the job is
jokes.explanation: Bart's characteristic bluntness mixed with genuine appreciation for his father's sacrifice.

jokes.text: Thirteen bucks? Hey, wait a minute. That's right. $120 gross. Less Social Security, less unemployment insurance, less Santa training, less costume purchase, less beard rental, less Christmas club.
jokes.setup: Homer receiving his mall Santa paycheck
jokes.punchline: Absurd list of deductions reducing pay to almost nothing
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalating list of ridiculous workplace deductions - relatable corporate exploitation taken to absurd extreme.

jokes.text: Thirteen bucks? you can't get anything for 13 bucks. Allright! Thirteen bigones! Springfield Downs, here I come!
jokes.setup: Homer despairing over tiny paycheck
jokes.punchline: Barney's misguided enthusiasm about gambling with the small amount
jokes.explanation: Character contrast - Homer's despair vs. Barney's delusional optimism about the same small amount.

jokes.text: I may be a total washout as a father, but I'm not gonna take my kid... to a sleazy dog track on Christmas Eve.
jokes.setup: Homer showing parental responsibility
jokes.punchline: Setting a very low bar for good parenting
jokes.explanation: Homer's self-awareness about his failures while trying to maintain minimal standards.

jokes.text: If TV has taught me anything, it's that miracles always happen to poor kids at Christmas. It happened to Tiny Tim, it happened to Charlie Brown, it happened to the Smurfs and it's gonna happen to us.
jokes.setup: Bart encouraging father to gamble
jokes.punchline: List of fictional Christmas miracles including the Smurfs
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-commentary on Christmas TV specials with perfect escalation from classic to absurd examples.

jokes.text: Well, okay, let's go. Who's Tiny Tim?
jokes.setup: Homer agreeing to gambling plan
jokes.punchline: Homer's cultural ignorance about classic Christmas story
jokes.explanation: Homer's characteristic lack of cultural knowledge, even about famous Christmas stories.

jokes.text: Bart, did you hear that? What a name! Santa's Little Helper! It's a sign! It's an omen! It's a coincidence, Dad.
jokes.setup: Dog named Santa's Little Helper entering race
jokes.punchline: Homer's superstitious excitement vs. Bart's rational response
jokes.explanation: Character reversal - child being more rational than adult about superstitious thinking.

jokes.text: Wow! Ninety-nine times thirteen equals... Merry Christmas!
jokes.setup: Homer calculating potential winnings
jokes.punchline: Skipping the math to jump to the optimistic conclusion
jokes.explanation: Homer's characteristic avoidance of actual work (math) in favor of wishful thinking.

jokes.text: Well, I wish you wouldn't... because aside from the fact he has the same frailties as all human belings. he's the only father I have. Therefore, he is my model of manhood, and my estimation of him will govern the prospects of my adult relationships.
jokes.setup: Aunt Patty criticizing Homer
jokes.punchline: Lisa's sophisticated psychological defense of her father
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment - Lisa's adult-level psychological insight delivered by an 8-year-old defending her father.

jokes.text: Mm-hmm. Go watch your cartoon show, dear.
jokes.setup: Lisa's sophisticated defense of Homer
jokes.punchline: Patty dismissing her as just a child despite the mature argument
jokes.explanation: Adult's dismissive response to child's wisdom - classic failure to recognize insight from unexpected source.

jokes.text: Doesn't seem possible, but I guess TV has betrayed me.
jokes.setup: Homer's dog losing the race despite the perfect name
jokes.punchline: Blaming television for his false expectations about Christmas miracles
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback to Bart's TV miracle theory with Homer's disappointed realization about media influence.

jokes.text: But he's a loser! He's pathetic! He's-- A Simpson.
jokes.setup: Homer describing the losing racing dog
jokes.punchline: Realizing the dog's failures match his own family
jokes.explanation: Brilliant self-aware moment where Homer realizes the dog's failures mirror his own family's struggles - perfect economy and emotional resonance.

jokes.text: I didn't get my Christmas bonus. I tried not to let it ruin Christmas for eveybody, but no matter what I did--
jokes.setup: Homer finally confessing to family
jokes.punchline: Admitting all his schemes have failed
jokes.explanation: Homer's vulnerable confession moment - more touching than funny but important character development.

jokes.text: So love at first sight is possible. And if he runs away, he'll be easy to catch.
jokes.setup: Family bonding with new dog
jokes.punchline: Practical observation about the slow dog's capture-ability
jokes.explanation: Bart's practical assessment of their new pet's limitations based on racing performance.

jokes.text: Like a light bulb! Bart! Like Shnozzola! Lisa! Like strippoker! I'm warning you two!
jokes.setup: Family singing Christmas carols together
jokes.punchline: Kids adding inappropriate additions to Rudolph song
jokes.explanation: Classic Simpsons family moment - kids subverting wholesome activity while Homer tries to maintain order.

jokes.text: Like Attila the Hu-- You little--
jokes.setup: Bart continuing to add inappropriate historical references
jokes.punchline: Homer finally snapping and chasing Bart
jokes.explanation: Classic ending - Homer's patience finally breaking and chasing Bart for his irreverence.

tmdb_overview: Homer's Christmas bonus is canceled and the rainy-day funds are needed to pay for Bart's tattoo removal.
insight: Pilot establishes three joke types in 22 minutes, landing 69 gags despite uneven craft marks.
